pattern: Seating the next student to maximize distance to the nearest neighbor only ever needs to compare the two open ends of the row against the widest gap between two already-seated students, so a sorted list of occupied seats is enough state.
hints: Keep the occupied seats in sorted order; the only candidates for the next seat are the two ends of the row and the midpoint of each gap between consecutive occupied seats.

The distance earned by sitting at the left end is the position of the first occupied seat itself, and at the right end is (n - 1) minus the position of the last occupied seat; for an internal gap between seats a and b, the earned distance is (b - a) // 2 and the seat chosen is a + that amount.

Scan all candidates, keep the one with the largest earned distance breaking ties toward the smaller seat number, then insert it into the sorted list; leave() just removes that seat from the sorted list.

edge_inputs.description: first three seats spread to the two ends and the widest remaining gap before any departures
a departure reopens a gap that becomes the next best seat
capacity of one seats only 0, ever
capacity of two forces alternating between the two ends
an odd-width gap must floor its midpoint, choosing the lower of the two equidistant seats
repeated leave/seat cycles at the same two seats must not drift the state

stress.description: long randomized mix of seat and leave calls against a small room
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import bisect
    n = rng.randint(2, 30)
    ops = ['ExamRoom']
    args = [[n]]
    students = []
    def choose_seat():
        if not students:
            return 0
        best_seat, best_dist = 0, students[0]
        for i in range(len(students) - 1):
            l, r = students[i], students[i + 1]
            d = (r - l) // 2
            if d > best_dist:
                best_dist = d
                best_seat = l + d
        end_dist = n - 1 - students[-1]
        if end_dist > best_dist:
            best_seat = n - 1
        return best_seat
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        if students and rng.random() < 0.4:
            p = rng.choice(students)
            students.remove(p)
            ops.append('leave')
            args.append([p])
        else:
            if len(students) >= n:
                continue
            seat = choose_seat()
            bisect.insort(students, seat)
            ops.append('seat')
            args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: the generator mirrors the gap-selection algorithm itself so leave() is always called with a seat that is genuinely occupied

complexity.time: O(m) per seat/leave, m = seats occupied
